claims.claimText: Serco has held the MoJ Electronic Monitoring contract continuously since 2014, giving it deep operational knowledge of tagging hardware, offender compliance workflows, and MoJ governance contacts.
The MoJ has historically extended Electronic Monitoring contracts rather than re-competing them, indicating high buyer inertia and low appetite for transition risk.
Serco's UK public sector revenue is approximately 95% of its UK divisional revenue, making retention of MoJ contracts a high-priority commercial imperative for Serco.

executiveJudgement.summary: Serco is a well-entrenched incumbent on the MoJ Electronic Monitoring contract [INC-CLM-001]. Buyer inertia is high [INC-CLM-002] and Serco has a strong commercial incentive to defend [INC-CLM-003].
executiveJudgement.rationale: Displacement is feasible only with clear transition evidence and a superior commercial offer.

sources.title: Supplier dossier — Serco Group plc v1.0.0
Buyer dossier — Ministry of Justice v1.0.0
